data.title: Koenigsegg Jesko v Lambo Revuelto v 1000hp BMW M3 DRAG RACE_transcript

paragraphs.context: koenigsegg jesko v lambo revuelto v 1000hp bmw m3 drag race _ transcript what will happen in a drag race between a lamborghini roalto, a koisgco attack, and a rather special bmw m3 touring? well, we ' re going to find out cuz i ' m going to race them over the standing quarter mile. i ' m matt watson, and you ' re watching car wow. the lamborghini roalto has a 6.5 l naturally aspirated v12 with three electric motors, one in the gearbox and two on the front axle for all - wheel drive. combined, you have 1,015 horsepower and 162 new m of torque. it has an 8 - speed dual clutch automatic gearbox with launch control. the car weighs in at 1,872 kilos and costs from around £450, 000. the koenig yesco attack has a 5 l twin turbo v8 that produces 1,280 horsepower and 1,000 nm of torque when running on superleted fuel. that power is sent to the rear wheels only via a 9 - speed multiclutch automatic gearbox with launch control. the car weighs in at 1,420 kg and costs around £5 million. this bmw m3 touring has been tuned by gtech motorworks. it has a fully built 3 l twinturbo straight 6 with a new exhaust and now makes 1,000 horsepower and 1,100 nm of torque. it has all - wheel drive and an 8 - speed automatic gearbox with launch control. the car weighs in at 1,865 kg and costs, including all upgrades, come to about £150, 000. right, this is going to be really interesting. i don ' t know what ' s going to happen. you can never tell with tune cars. that bmw, it ' s a complete wild card. could perform, could break. koigse egg, it ' s all going to be about traction. rear wheel drive. we ' re not running it on e85, so it ' s not as fast as a yesco can be, but still got lots of horsepower. it ' s got the most horsepower here, but it ' s got the least driven wheels. and then we ' ve got this four - wheel drive, plenty of performance, should just deliver numbers day in day out, but we ' ll find out. let ' s speak to the owners first, though
